en: [Music]
The very first version of this movie
started out um in the Old West with a
western opening. The very first pass
from the the original treatment was more
of this kind of spaghetti western
classic Woody the lone cowboy riding on
Bullseye comes into town. Buzz Lightyear
from space and he lands and they have a
one-on-one confrontation and we would see all of our toys up in the windows
and then the Slinky was in the water
trough hiding. That idea came from what
was also originally in the script is Woody and Buzz actually had more of a of
a face off during the prison escape that
Buzz being a deluded minion of Lato
still under his power would try to stop
Woody and they would have a battle. And
the original concept was Woody uses the
power of friendship to make this diluted
Buzz turn back to him himself and realize that I've been switched over.
And so the real buzz inside him, the kernel of real buzz flipped him over,
which wasn't a bad idea. Through the
course of developing the movie, that
went away. So this kind of holdover of
this slow paced kind of western opening
didn't necessarily need to be in the movie. And we realized, God, it would be
so much cooler if we just started this movie off with a bang. We just really
went in there and really had something that just had a ton of energy, had a ton
of excitement, showed how cool it is to see these toys, and to recognize that
it's been a lot of years since people have had a chance to see Buzz and Woody,
let's just do something that's really awesome. So, we went back to the drawing
board and realized, okay, if we scrap
this kind of classic slow pace western
opening, what can we do? And the idea
came up of, well, let's realize what
Andy's Playtime is. What does it feel
like to be played with as a toy? Let's
fully realize an Andy playtime. Let's
recall the past playtime. So, we use
dialogue from old Andy Playtime. And the
first first incarnation after this kind
of western showdown was the very first line was the very first line from Toy
Story 1 was, "This is a stickup. Don't
anybody move." All right, everyone. This
is a stickup. Don't anybody move. And
so, if people got it and were super Toy Story geeks, they got it and they liked
it. If people didn't, it would work as its own. And that's what drove us. And
so that gave us, you know, Slinky with a force field and a giant dinosaur that
eats forcefield dogs. Then we started adding stuff. We started adding crazy
stuff like Mrs. Potato Head being a
ninja, the pink Corvette as a getaway
car, the exploding bridge, and and then
we kind of started to expand. So it
became a version of Playtime. Some of
which we seen, and some of which we just happened offcreen.
